faqs.question: How much does new siding cost in Seattle?
faqs.answer: Siding replacement in Seattle typically ranges from $8,000–$25,000 depending on home size, material choice, and condition of the substrate. James Hardie fiber cement runs $12–18/sq ft installed; vinyl runs $6–10/sq ft. Request a free on-site estimate for an exact quote.

faqs.question: What siding is best for Seattle's rainy climate?
faqs.answer: James Hardie fiber cement is the top recommendation for Seattle. It cannot rot, won't absorb moisture, and resists moss — addressing the three biggest siding failure modes in the Pacific Northwest. Cedar is beautiful but requires ongoing maintenance. Vinyl is low-maintenance but can become brittle in cold snaps.

faqs.question: How long does siding installation take?
faqs.answer: A typical single-family home takes 3–7 days from demo to completion, depending on home size, material, and weather. Permit filing adds 1–3 weeks of lead time in Seattle and most suburban municipalities.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit to replace siding in Seattle?
faqs.answer: Yes. The City of Seattle requires a permit for full siding replacement. We handle all permit applications with Seattle DCI and suburban city permit offices as part of our service.

faqs.question: Is James Hardie worth the extra cost over vinyl?
faqs.answer: For owner-occupied Seattle homes, yes. Hardie costs 30–50% more than vinyl upfront but carries a 30-year warranty, holds paint 15+ years, and carries significantly higher home resale value. Vinyl is the better choice for rental properties and investment homes on tight budgets.
